content: Just as the language learning definition of reflection frames it as a core metacognitive step to turn passive language input into intuitive mastery by matching produced output to native usage norms, the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework can borrow this educational framing to improve LLM self-reflection: LLM self-reflection should not just check for logical errors, but actively align its generated outputs against implicit human communication norms stored in long-term dynamic memory, similar to how language learners refine their output through repeated reflective practice. This turns LLM self-reflection from a post-hoc error correction step into an incremental skill-building process identical to human language learning.
